articles.title: Oil supply crunch intensifies as last Hormuz tankers reach refineries
articles.summary: Final prewar cargoes to leave Gulf set to unload in coming days, escalating a global rush to secure barrels

articles.title: Trump says Iran has made contact and wants deal ‘very badly’
articles.summary: President confirms naval blockade of Strait of Hormuz has officially come into effect

articles.title: Mediators pursue Iran-US deal in back-channel diplomacy
articles.summary: Trump says Iran representatives have ‘called’ and want a deal ‘very badly’

articles.title: Why time is on Iran’s side
articles.summary: A global energy crisis is only just beginning. Political turmoil will follow

articles.title: Meta builds AI version of Mark Zuckerberg to interact with staff
articles.summary: CEO is training and testing his own character as part of wider push to develop ‘personal superintelligence’

articles.title: Trump deletes ‘Jesus’ post after backlash from allies
articles.summary: US president circulated image of himself as Christ-like figure shortly after lashing out at Pope Leo

articles.title: Goldman bond traders take shine off bumper quarter
articles.summary: Equities unit powers bank’s earnings, while fixed income, currencies and commodities business fall well short

articles.title: Goldman Sachs retains its knack for spinning anxiety into gold
articles.summary: AI-inspired stock routs, the war in Iran and private credit were no hindrance to the Wall Street bank

articles.title: Europe’s far right questions merits of Trump ties after Orbán defeat
articles.summary: Alternative for Germany quarrels about Maga after Hungarian election fiasco

articles.title: Hungary’s election could support Europe’s revival trade
articles.summary: In the long run, the result may be felt far beyond Budapest

articles.title: SEC boss tells retail investors to ‘stay out of the kitchen’ on private credit
articles.summary: Paul Atkins tells audience at spring IMF meetings that $1.8tn lending sector ‘is not a systemic risk’

articles.title: Oil Gains as US Blocks Hormuz, Trump Says Iran Reached Out
articles.summary: Oil rose after a US blockade of Iranian ports threatened to tighten global markets further, though gains were capped on signs that truce talks could still be active.
articles.author: Will Kubzansky, Alex Longley

articles.title: Treasuries Rise on Oil Pullback as Trump Flags Iran Deal Talks
articles.summary: US Treasuries advanced after President Donald Trump said Iranian officials were seeking an agreement on terms to end the war, triggering a pullback in oil prices and a drop in the dollar.
articles.author: Michael MacKenzie, James Hirai

articles.title: Inside the $20T ETF Industry, 'QQQ' Competition Heats Up | ETF IQ 4/13/2026
articles.summary: "Bloomberg ETF IQ" focuses on the opportunities, risks and current trends tied to the trillions of dollars in the global exchange traded funds industry. Today's guests: Laffer Tengler CEO & CIO Nancy Tengler, ETF.com President & Director of Research Dave Nadig, and Breakwave Advisors Managing Partner John Kartsonas. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Oncoclinicas to File for Creditor Protection on Monday
articles.summary: Oncoclinicas do Brasil Servicos Medicos SA said it will file for temporary court protection in Brazil on Monday as it faces mounting financial pressures.
articles.author: Giovanna Bellotti Azevedo, Rachel Gamarski

articles.title: BlackRock Pivots Back to US Stocks With Strategists Seeing Iran War ‘Contained’
articles.summary: BlackRock strategists have returned to an overweight view on US stocks as they see the damage to global economic growth from the Middle East conflict “likely contained.”
articles.author: Felice Maranz

articles.title: Stocks Extend Gains as Trump Says Iran Wants Deal: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Stocks stood near session highs while oil prices pared earlier gains after President Donald Trump said Iran still wanted to make a deal following a deadlock in peace talks and a US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. Meanwhile, Goldman Sachs Group Inc. shares dropped in an underwhelming start to the earnings season.
articles.author: Ira Iosebashvili, Vildana Hajric, Isabelle Lee

articles.title: SpaceX IPO to Be Crowded Trade: Dave Nadig
articles.summary: Dave Nadig, president and director of research at ETF.com, joins Katie Greifeld and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." Investment bankers are hitting the road to raise more than $15 billion across initial public offerings in the coming weeks, nervously eyeing market volatility as a standoff with Iran threatens a fragile ceasefire. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US Natural Gas Erases Gains as Traders Eye Forecasts, Storage
articles.summary: US natural gas futures ended down for the fourth consecutive session, erasing earlier gains as traders weighed forecasts for mild weather and a growing surplus of domestic stockpiles relative to the five-year average.
articles.author: Julian Hast

articles.title: Blocking Hormuz Won’t Work for Trump
articles.summary: Trump’s blockade of the Strait of Hormuz is likely to drag the US deeper into war, instead of toward diplomacy, explains Bloomberg Opinion columnist Marc Champion. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: UN Warns of Food Risks From Fertilizer, Energy Trade Curbs
articles.summary: The United Nations urged countries not to limit shipments of energy and fertilizers as the conflict in the Middle East deepens, warning that such restrictions in the past have exacerbated spikes in global food prices.
articles.author: Ilena Peng

articles.title: The Manager Behind the Best-Performing ETF of 2026
articles.summary: John Kartsonas, founder and managing partner at Breakwave Advisors, joins Katie Greifeld and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." Every twist in the Iran conflict shows up almost instantly in the Breakwave Tanker Shipping ETF (ticker: BWET) that has become the best-performing ETF of 2026. The fund has gained over 1400% over the past year. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: What to Know About the US Blockade of Iran’s Ports
articles.summary: Frustrated by Iran’s unwillingness to give up its strangehold over the Strait of Hormuz, US President Donald Trump is trying to force the issue. The US military announced that it had imposed a full blockage of Iran’s southern coastline, including traffic from there through the Strait of Hormuz, as of April 13.
articles.author: Weilun Soon, Clara Ferreira Marques, Alex Longley

articles.title: Bloomberg Surveillance 4/13/2026
articles.summary: Jonathan Ferro, Lisa Abramowicz and Annmarie Hordern speak daily with leaders and decision makers from Wall Street to Washington and beyond. No other program better positions investors and executives for the trading day. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Goldman Shares Fall as Bond Trading Miss Outweighs Equity Record
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.’s surprise drop in bond- and rates-trading revenue cast a shadow over another record quarter from the bank’s equities team, prompting Chief Executive Officer David Solomon to point the finger at analysts for setting the bar so high.
articles.author: Todd Gillespie

articles.title: Two Months to get Oil Back Online is 'Optimistic,' Says Rebecca Babin
articles.summary: Rebecca Babin, senior equity trader and managing director at CIBC Private Wealth, said that the markets are using a two month base case as the predicted time it would take to get oil production back up and running after the end of war with Iran, a timeline she finds 'optimistic.' Babin said that even if US oil exports grows to 5 million barrels a day, it still won't be enough to fill in the supply lost to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Viking, Lone Pine Alumni Shutter $2 Billion Alua Hedge Fund
articles.summary: Alua Capital Management is closing its $2 billion hedge fund after about five years, concluding its returns were inadequate despite some periods of strong performance.
articles.author: Katherine Burton

articles.title: One-Off Hits Threaten Confidence in Private Credit, Bailey Warns
articles.summary: Private credit funds are facing higher risks from one-off hits that could threaten confidence in the wider sector, Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey warned on Monday.
articles.author: Laura Noonan

articles.title: Hormuz Strait Halts 'Extorting the Global Economy,' Says Former State Dept. Official
articles.summary: Heather Conley, senior fellow at the American Enterprise Institute, said the US blockade of Iran in the Strait of Hormuz is a temporary measure and won't remove all threats to shipping in the area. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: US Hospitals Are Emerging From Bankruptcy Only to Falter Again
articles.summary: The hospitals that emerged from the bankruptcies of Steward Health Care System and Prospect Medical Holdings Inc. were supposed to help stabilize one of the world’s largest hospital landlords by restoring a battered tenant base after two years of industry stress.
articles.author: Soma Biswas

articles.title: Goldman Sachs Extends Borrowing Run With $6.5 Billion Bond Sale
articles.summary: Goldman Sachs Group Inc. is selling $6.5 billion of US investment-grade notes on Monday, extending a borrowing spree that included a record debt offering earlier this year.
articles.author: Brian W Smith, Gerson Freitas Jr

articles.title: Gold Eases as US Plan to Blockade Hormuz Raises Inflation Risks
articles.summary: Gold edged down amid mounting inflation concerns after US President Donald Trump’s order to blockade the Strait of Hormuz deepened the global energy-supply shock.
articles.author: Yihui Xie, Preeti Soni

articles.title: Dollar Flips to Losses After Trump Says Iran Wants a Deal
articles.summary: The dollar erased gains after President Donald Trump said Iran called Monday morning and wants a deal. The remarks follow Trump’s order over the weekend to block the Strait of Hormuz following the deadlock in US-Iran peace talks that pushed oil prices higher.
articles.author: Anya Andrianova

articles.title: Sundheim’s D1 Hedge Fund Tumbled 6% in March as Biggest Stock Bets Soured
articles.summary: D1 Capital Partners’ equities book tumbled 6% in March, according to people familiar with the matter, making Dan Sundheim’s firm one of the worst-performing stock-pickers that month.
articles.author: Hema Parmar

articles.title: Austin Seeks $1 Billion of Munis for Airport Expansion Plan
articles.summary: Austin is set to bring a $1.18 billion airport revenue bond sale to market on Tuesday as growth in one of the nation’s fastest-expanding metro areas strains capacity at Austin-Bergstrom International Airport.
articles.author: Aashna Shah

articles.title: Oil Prices Will Soon Converge to Reflect Crisis, IEA Says
articles.summary: Oil prices don’t yet reflect the severity of the unprecedented supply crisis caused by the Iran war, but they soon will, the head of the International Energy Agency said.
articles.author: Grant Smith

articles.title: Aluminum Jumps to Four-Year High on Trump’s Blockade of Hormuz
articles.summary: Aluminum jumped to a four-year high in London, as US President Donald Trump’s blockade of Iranian ports threatened more disruptions to shipments from the Persian Gulf.
articles.author: Martin Ritchie

articles.title: Countries That Bet On China Are Winning
articles.summary: Trump may have finally lost Asia to China over the Iran war. Bloomberg Opinion columnist Mihir Sharma explains. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Why Private Credit Is Facing a Sudden Investor Exodus
articles.summary: SaaSpocalypse. Credit defaults. The war in Iran. A lot of worries are hitting the $1.8 trillion private credit market at the same time, setting off a scramble by some investors to withdraw money from the industry’s giants. In recent weeks, funds managed by firms such as Apollo Global Management Inc., BlackRock Inc. and Ares Management Corp. have faced unprecedented requests for redemptions — and, in many cases, have exercised their right to block investors from getting all their money out.
articles.author: Paula Seligson

articles.title: US Stocks Resume Gains, Close to Erasing Iran War-Driven Losses
articles.summary: US equities rose for the eighth time in nine sessions as investors assessed the US Naval blockade of the Strait of Hormuz that took effect Monday and parsed through earnings announcements at home.
articles.author: Geoffrey Morgan

articles.title: Venezuela Set to Award Oil Fields to Chevron to Help Grow Output
articles.summary: Chevron Corp. is set to expand its oil footprint in Venezuela, benefitting from its almost 20-year bet to remain in the country amid political and economic turmoil.
articles.author: Fabiola Zerpa, Eric Martin, Kevin Crowley
